diagrams.title: OSI vs TCP/IP Model Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing mapping between 7-layer OSI model and 4-layer TCP/IP model
elements: OSI 7 Layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application
TCP/IP 4 Layers: Network Access, Internet, Transport, Application
Mapping lines: OSI Physical+DataLink → TCP/IP Network Access; OSI Network → TCP/IP Internet; OSI Transport → TCP/IP Transport; OSI Session+Presentation+Application → TCP/IP Application
Color coding to show equivalent layers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding correspondence between theoretical OSI model and practical TCP/IP protocol suite

diagrams.title: OSI Encapsulation Process
diagrams.description: Shows how data is encapsulated as it moves down the OSI layers from Application to Physical
elements: Arrows showing downward flow and header addition
Labels showing what each header contains (ports, IP addresses, MAC addresses)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how protocol control information is added at each layer and understanding data transformation

diagrams.title: PDU Types at Each OSI Layer
diagrams.description: Diagram showing Protocol Data Units at all seven OSI layers
elements: Horizontal arrows between layers showing encapsulation
diagrams.purpose: Memorizing PDU names for each layer, crucial for exam preparation

diagrams.title: Layer-Device Association
diagrams.description: Shows which network devices operate at which OSI layers
elements: Stack diagram showing devices at their respective layers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which devices operate at which layers and their role in networking
